title: Aluminium Prices Are On The Rise As US Premium At Record High
summary: This market update says aluminum prices are rising, with the U.S. premium at record highs. It notes a sharp increase in U.S. consumer pricing and says LME aluminum prices are also trading near three-year highs.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports that aluminum rose to 3,762 USD/T on June 2, 2026, up 0.81% from the previous day. It also shows the price is up 5.21% over the past month and 52.15% over the past year, indicating a strong upward trend.

title: Aluminium Price Today
summary: Investing.com lists current aluminum futures at 3,762.00, matching the latest market level shown in the search results. The page is oriented toward live pricing, charts, and market movement tracking rather than a narrative news report.

title: Aluminum Price (Monthly) - Historical Data & Trends
summary: YCharts shows aluminum at 3,666.10, up from 3,599.85 the prior month and 2,448.79 one year earlier. The data suggests a sustained upward price trend over both the short and long term.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Fox News reports that Century Aluminum decided to raise production to full capacity. The item ties the move to the broader policy and tariff environment affecting U.S. aluminum output.

title: Iran Strikes Gulf Aluminum Smelters, Disrupting Global Supply
summary: Exiger says missile and drone attacks damaged major aluminum smelters in Abu Dhabi and Bahrain, affecting production capacity rather than just logistics. The report says the outages could tighten already constrained global supply and push prices higher.

title: Credit Conditions: U.S. Aluminum Supply Chain Takes A Strait Hit
summary: S&P Global Ratings reports that aluminum prices surged as roughly 8% of global capacity was disrupted. It also notes that the U.S. Midwest transaction premium reached a record high.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process designed to reduce hazardous waste from aluminum production. In lab-scale tests, the membrane captured more than 99% of aluminum ions from simulated waste streams, with the goal of upcycling recovered aluminum and increasing yield.

title: US steel demand 'nowhere near' prior expectations
summary: MEPS International says U.S. steel demand has fallen well short of earlier expectations, while supply remains plentiful. The report also notes that flat steel prices were down and that tariffs and uncertainty are continuing to weigh on demand and pricing.

title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil ...
summary: Ryerson’s market update says steel prices are continuing to move higher across flat, plate, and long products, supported by constrained supply and improving demand. The article is focused on whether recent price strength may reverse, making it relevant to near-term market trend tracking.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports that steel prices fell to 3,183 CNY/T on June 3, 2026, down 0.22% from the previous day. The page also shows that prices are down over the past month but remain higher year over year, indicating mixed short-term and medium-term momentum.

title: Steel Production Dips in the U.S. | #ScrapMonsterNews
summary: The video reports that weekly U.S. raw steel production fell by about 2.4%, with output at 1.656 million net tons. It also says capacity utilization declined from 76.2% to 74.4%, indicating softer near-term steel production conditions.

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: The article says U.S. steel production was 4.1% above the year-to-date average for the week of May 10, 2025, and 1.8% above the same week in 2024. It also reports mill capability utilization at 77.5%, suggesting some stabilization in production and capacity use.

title: Industry Data
summary: AISI reports that domestic raw steel production in the week ending May 30, 2026 was 1,872,000 net tons. The capability utilization rate was 81.1%, making this the most directly relevant recent capacity and production update in the search results.

title: Copper Prices Crash $0.20! Trade Shock Sends Scrappers ...
summary: This video says copper prices fell by $0.20 per pound after a new European trade agreement announcement. It frames the move as a volatile market shock affecting scrap metal pricing and notes the U.S. COMEX copper price near 558 cents per pound.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports that copper futures fell below $6.6 per pound on Wednesday, while still remaining close to the previous session’s record high. The update attributes the move to uncertainty over U.S. trade conditions and broader market positioning.

title: Copper Futures Price Today
summary: Investing.com shows copper futures at 6.5933, below the previous close of 6.6765. This reflects a pullback in the metal’s price, consistent with a volatile short-term trading environment.

title: Latest Copper News
summary: NewsNow aggregates recent copper-related headlines, including coverage of prices stabilizing and mining-company share moves tied to copper market conditions. The page is a news index rather than a single article, but it highlights current market attention on the commodity.

title: Copper Archives - MINING.COM
summary: MINING.COM’s copper archive shows a June 2 item on CopperTech Metals’ revenue surge in its US IPO filing. The snippet indicates the copper producer is preparing to list on the New York Stock Exchange, which is relevant to copper production and sector capacity activity.

title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
summary: Fastmarkets’ copper page highlights recent reporting on copper producers posting stronger first-quarter 2026 earnings amid elevated copper prices. It also notes concentrate output trends, making it relevant to production and capacity conditions in the copper market.

title: Copper near record highs
summary: MarketPulse reports copper futures rising above USD 6.29 per pound near record highs, supported by expectations of long-term demand and supply constraints. While primarily a price story, it directly relates to production capacity pressures in the copper market.

title: Record copper price signals accelerating race for supplies
summary: Reuters reports copper prices surging to records above $13,000 a metric ton amid fears of shortages and expectations of tighter supply. The article is not from the last 24 hours, but it is directly relevant to production constraints and capacity concerns in copper markets.

title: Copper must scale up production within sustainable limits
summary: The article says the copper industry faces a dual challenge: increasing production to meet demand while reducing its environmental footprint. It frames sustainability, efficiency, and responsible scaling as central to the sector's future.

title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
summary: EY reports that copper companies are responding to rising demand by expanding production while also prioritizing sustainability and digital innovation. The piece links copper demand to the broader energy transition and the need for more resilient supply strategies.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: This article highlights copper's importance to clean energy systems and notes that demand could nearly double by 2050. It emphasizes that recycling, resource efficiency, and technological innovation are key to keeping copper supply sustainable.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: S&P Global discusses how energy transition demand and digitalization are driving long-term copper consumption, including demand from AI and data centers. The report also points to recycling growth as an important sustainability lever for meeting future needs.

title: The role of copper in the energy transition
summary: DNV explains that copper is essential for solar, wind, power cables, and energy storage, but supply constraints could slow the transition. The article recommends recycling, resource diversification, technology innovation, and collaboration to support sustainable copper supply.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel, described as the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been told to drastically cut output. The company is said to have received a 12 million-ton ore production quota for 2026, down from 42 million tons in 2025, and intends to apply for a revision.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: S&P Global reports that Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue. The article says the move also reflects a broader policy shift to regulate supply volatility and crack down on environmentally harmful operations.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article examines how optimizing nickel production can support a more sustainable battery supply chain and a net-zero future. It emphasizes ESG integration, responsible investment, stakeholder engagement, and stronger ESG reporting as key responses to the environmental challenges facing the nickel industry.

title: Q&A: Experts argue clean energy must secure a sustainable supply of nickel
summary: This Q&A discusses why nickel is increasingly important to the clean energy economy and why supply-chain sustainability is becoming a major concern. It references a Joule paper arguing that clean energy demand must be paired with a sustainable nickel supply.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: This Nickel Institute blog page introduces commentary and articles focused on sustainability, treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production. It appears to be a broader topical hub rather than a single news article.

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
summary: This article looks at innovations in mining and refining that aim to reduce costs while improving the environmental performance of nickel production. It frames sustainable nickel production as both an industrial and economic opportunity.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali announced it has signed a contract to acquire a 65% controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, a stainless steel scrap business in Italy. The transaction was expected to close in the coming months, and the move was framed as an upstream integration step.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali sourcing all energy from renewables
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali is reported to be sourcing all of its power needs in Europe from renewable energy. The article also says the company plans to build a hydroelectric plant and an electrolyzer at its main site in Aosta.
